meta.title: Unit 3 — Pronouns & I/me (Test 7)
meta.subject: English Grammar
meta.description: Advanced pronoun practice with subject/object distinction and sentence construction.

questions.question: Which pronoun is always capitalized?
questions.explanation: The pronoun "I" is always written as a capital letter.

questions.question: "She" is used for female people.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'She' replaces a female person's name (e.g., Amy → She).

questions.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: Me have a class every Monday.
I have a class every Monday.
My have a class every Monday.
Mine have a class every Monday.
questions.explanation: "I have" — 'I' is the subject before the verb 'have'.

questions.question: "They" can replace a group of animals.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'They' replaces plural nouns — including groups of animals.

questions.question: Mina's dad is surprised. → ___ is surprised.
questions.explanation: Mina's dad is male → use 'He'.

questions.question: Fill in: ___ am eight years old. (I or me)
questions.explanation: 'I' is the subject — it comes before the verb. I am eight years old.

questions.question: How big does a wolf grow? → How big does ___ grow?
questions.explanation: "a wolf" is a singular animal → use 'it'.

questions.question: Sort these pronouns: which come BEFORE the verb (subject)?
questions.explanation: I/she/we/they = subject pronouns. me = object pronoun.

questions.question: In the sentence "She always helps me with homework," the pronoun 'me' is correct.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'me' comes after the verb 'helps' (as the object). This is correct.

questions.question: Emma and I see a snake. → ___ see a snake. (one word)
questions.explanation: Emma and I = two people (including the speaker) → use 'We'.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct: "Rosa gives me the basket." 'me' is object after the verb.

questions.question: Which pronoun is used in this sentence? "Spotty is my dog. It likes to play fetch."
questions.explanation: "It" is the pronoun — it replaces 'Spotty' (a dog = animal).

questions.question: The sentence "Rosa and I paint it" uses pronouns correctly.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'I' is part of the subject 'Rosa and I' (before verb). 'it' replaces an object.

questions.question: Which sentence uses 'me' correctly?
options: Can you give me the pencil?
questions.explanation: "give me" — 'me' is correctly used as the object after the verb 'give'.

questions.question: Fill in: My mom calls ___ every day. (I or me)
questions.explanation: 'me' is the object — it comes after the verb. My mom calls me.

questions.question: Sort these: which blank needs 'I', and which needs 'me'?
questions.explanation: 'I' = subject (before verb). 'me' = object (after verb or preposition).

questions.question: Which sentence is CORRECT?
options: I have so much fun in the desert.
questions.explanation: "I have so much fun" — 'I' is the subject before the verb. All others need 'me' instead.

questions.question: In the sentence "Wolves don't eat people, so we are not in danger," the pronoun 'we' is correct.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'we' includes the speaker and other people — it's used correctly here.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct: "We go to the library." 'We' is the subject pronoun.

questions.question: Which sentence has the WRONG pronoun?
options: I have a class every Monday.
Me go to school by bus.
questions.explanation: "Me go" is wrong — 'Me' cannot be the subject. Correct: "I go to school by bus."
